text: The surgeon lifts the small bowel out of the pelvis, revealing a deep prolapse. The sacral uterine ligaments are visible, and the ureter is identified as the peritoneum is opened along the sacral promontory. The left iliac vein is noted nearby. The peritoneum is incised, and the cul-de-sac is redressed to reduce the prolapse. A tunnel is created behind the vagina, visible as the assistant lifts the vagina. A non-resorbable polypropylene mesh strip is positioned and tailored for protection. The anterior aspect of the rectum is sutured to the mesh to prevent further intussusception, with visible details of the suturing process.

The surgeon applies glue deeper in the recto-vaginal septum to expedite the procedure. The mesh is fixed to the sacral promontory without undue traction, avoiding the hypogastric nerve. An additional suture is placed to prevent further intussusception, and the peritoneal flaps are firmly closed over the mesh, completing the procedure.
